_README: Reference snippet for OpenLens 'kind-mirador-local' cluster preferences.
OpenLens stores its per-cluster preferences in a host-local file:
~/Library/Application Support/OpenLens/lens-cluster-store.json
That file is gitignored (host-specific paths, generated by OpenLens).
This snippet is the GOLDEN reference of what the kind-mirador-local
cluster's `preferences` block must look like for the Metrics tab to
work against our two observability stacks.
The script `bin/cluster/openlens/prometheus-config.sh` reads which
overlay is currently deployed (lgtm-only vs kube-prom-stack) and
writes the right block automatically.
Two valid preferences shapes — the script picks one based on cluster state:
A. lgtm-only (default `local/` overlay) — provider 'lens' + Mimir at infra/lgtm:9009
B. kube-prom-stack (`local-prom/` overlay) — provider 'operator' + monitoring/prometheus-stack-kube-prom-prometheus:9090
Why provider matters: the OpenLens UI dropdown labels are misleading.
- 'Lens' = generic Prometheus URL mode (works with any /api/v1/query endpoint)
- 'Prometheus' = unspecified, often fails detection
- 'Helm' = Lens's own helm chart (not what we use)
- 'Stacklight' = Mirantis-specific (not us)
- 'Prometheus Operator' (provider value: 'operator') = kube-prometheus-stack
Verified empirically 2026-04-21: provider='prometheus' returned
metadata.prometheus.success=false even with correct service URL;
provider='operator' lit up Metrics tab immediately.
